answer.text: The rate of change of delta with respect to change in the price of the underlying asset
The rate of change of option premium with respect to time
The rate of change of premium with respect to volatility
The rate of change of premium with respect to interest rates

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Gamma = Change in delta / Unit change in underlying. It's the 'acceleration' of the option — how fast delta changes. Gamma is highest for ATM options and decreases for deep ITM/OTM. **The Trap:** Gamma is the SECOND derivative of option price with respect to underlying price (delta is the first). **Cross-Exam Context:** Gamma concept is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** High gamma means delta changes rapidly with small price moves — making the option harder to delta-hedge.
